profile.name: Zhiping Lan
profile.tagline: Senior Software Engineer | Tech Lead | Scalable Web Systems
profile.bio:
ExpediaGroup | GE HealthCare | EPAM Systems | Teleopti

A senior software engineer and tech lead with 10+ years of experience building scalable web applications and leading technical initiatives across travel, healthcare, retail, workforce management, and AI-related products.

Throughout my career, I've had the opportunity to work with leading organizations including ExpediaGroup, GE HealthCare, EPAM Systems, Teleopti, and FDT AI, where I've built and launched partner platforms, enterprise dashboards, medical-device asset systems, e-commerce experiences, scheduling products, and internal tooling that empowered multiple teams.

My expertise spans AWS Cloud, CI/CD pipelines, React and TypeScript ecosystems, frontend architecture, technical mentoring, and collaborative partnerships with product and design teams to deliver impactful solutions.

recentBlogs.title: Building a Shared Charting Library Across Multiple Expedia Teams
recentBlogs.summary: How a reusable charting library reduced duplication, standardized data visualization, and improved delivery speed across internal teams.
recentBlogs.content:
Context

At Expedia Group, I built a custom charting library that was adopted across multiple teams. The original problem was repeated effort: teams were solving similar data visualization needs with inconsistent implementations, styling, and maintenance costs.

What I Want To Expand Later

The design constraints that pushed us toward a shared library
The API design decisions that helped adoption
How we balanced flexibility with consistency

Draft Story

The core goal was not just to build components, but to create a reliable internal platform for product teams shipping metrics-heavy experiences. By packaging shared charting patterns into a reusable library, we reduced duplication and made dashboards easier to build and maintain.

This project also created a common language for frontend teams. Instead of rebuilding chart behavior for each surface, teams could focus on business logic and product outcomes.

Placeholder For Results

I want to add more concrete adoption numbers, before-and-after implementation examples, and lessons learned from rollout.

recentBlogs.title: Leading Frontend Architecture for a Rate Automation System
recentBlogs.summary: A draft write-up on leading frontend architecture for a large product initiative in Expedia PartnerCentral.
recentBlogs.content:
Context

I led the frontend architecture for a rate automation system in Expedia PartnerCentral. The product area focused on helping partners manage pricing workflows more effectively inside a large-scale platform serving Expedia's global hotel partners.

What Happened

This initiative required balancing product flexibility, architecture consistency, and long-term maintainability. As a technical lead, I worked across implementation details and broader system direction while partnering closely with PMs and UX designers.

Placeholder Details To Fill In

The product problem and target user flow
The main technical decisions behind the frontend architecture
Performance, adoption, or operational impact after launch
Lessons from coordinating across teams and legacy systems

Why This Matters

This was one of the clearest examples of combining technical leadership with product thinking. It was not only about writing code, but also about shaping direction, mentoring others, and keeping the system scalable as the initiative grew.

recentBlogs.title: Shared UI Platform and Internal NPM Package
recentBlogs.summary: Integrated a new UI toolkit into legacy systems through a reusable internal NPM package, improving consistency and easing adoption across teams.
recentBlogs.content: This work focused on modernization without forcing full rewrites. By wrapping the new UI toolkit in a reusable package, I helped teams adopt a more consistent component foundation while still working within legacy constraints.

recentBlogs.title: Executing a Zero-Downtime AWS Account Migration
recentBlogs.summary: A placeholder draft covering the zero-downtime migration of a core application across AWS accounts.
recentBlogs.content:
Migration Goal

One of my notable achievements at Expedia Group was executing a zero-downtime AWS account migration for a core application. The challenge was to preserve system reliability while changing an important piece of the operational foundation.

Draft Focus Areas

Migration planning and rollout strategy
Risk reduction and fallback planning
Coordination across engineering and platform stakeholders
What made zero-downtime execution possible

Placeholder For More Detail

I want to add the architecture diagram, migration sequencing, and verification steps I used so this can become a stronger technical case study later.

recentBlogs.title: Building Asset Performance Management for Medical Devices
recentBlogs.summary: A draft post about building an asset performance management system in the healthcare technology domain.
recentBlogs.content:
Context

At GE HealthCare, I worked in the healthcare business unit and built an Asset Performance Management system for medical devices. This work sat at the intersection of enterprise software, reliability, and healthcare operations.

Why It Was Important

The project required careful attention to product quality, maintainability, and team collaboration. It also became a strong example of how I contributed beyond implementation by mentoring peers, interviewing candidates, and supporting onboarding.

Placeholder Details To Fill In

The user and business problem behind the platform
What the system helped teams monitor or improve
Key frontend or platform design choices
What I learned from working in a healthcare technology environment

pastProjects.title: Burberry E-commerce Experience
pastProjects.summary: Contributed to Burberry's e-commerce web application at EPAM Systems, with a focus on frontend delivery, performance improvement, and server-side rendering.
pastProjects.content: Key contributions included frontend development for the e-commerce experience, performance improvements through server-side rendering and image optimization, and team enablement through JavaScript fundamentals training, onboarding support, and interviews.

pastProjects.title: Expedia PartnerCentral
pastProjects.summary: Worked on Expedia PartnerCentral, a property management platform serving Expedia's global hotel partners, with contributions spanning dashboards, rate automation, competitiveness tooling, and platform migration work.
pastProjects.content:
Expedia PartnerCentral is the strongest example of my recent work at scale. I contributed as both an individual engineer and a technical lead, partnering with PMs and UX designers, mentoring junior developers, helping shape architecture, and supporting team delivery processes.

Highlights from this work include a dynamic metrics dashboard, a shared charting library used across teams, a rate automation system, a partner competitiveness optimization system, and a zero-downtime AWS account migration of a core application.

pastProjects.title: GE HealthCare Asset Performance Management
pastProjects.summary: Built an Asset Performance Management system for medical devices in GE HealthCare's healthcare business unit.
pastProjects.content: This project combined enterprise engineering discipline with product reliability requirements in a healthcare environment. It also reflected my broader technical leadership through peer mentoring, interviewing, onboarding support, and steady delivery.

pastProjects.title: Teleopti Workforce Scheduling Tools
pastProjects.summary: Built enterprise scheduling tools at Teleopti, including seat planning and automated shift scheduling systems.
pastProjects.content: This work included AngularJS- and KnockoutJS-based product development, enterprise workflow design, product demos, and close collaboration with an international team using XP practices like pair programming.
